'''Frank Winterstein''' (born 17 December 1986) is a Samoa international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

Winterstein previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] and the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL), and for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Crusaders Rugby League]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]].

He played [[rugby union]] for the [[Kintetsu Liners]] in the [[Top League]] and the [[Australia national rugby sevens team|Australian rugby sevens team]].

==Background==
He was born in [[Canterbury, New South Wales|Canterbury]], New South Wales, Australia.

==Playing career==
Winterstein previously played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[NRL]].  Winterstein made his first grade debut for Canterbury-Bankstown in Round 13 2008 at [[ANZ Stadium]] against the [[Newcastle Knights]].  Winterstein made five appearances in his debut season as Canterbury endured a horror season on the field finishing last on the table and claimed the wooden spoon.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-wildcats-winterstein-poised-for-crusaders-1-2232538|title=WAKEFIELD WILDCATS: Winterstein poised for Crusaders|date=30 October 2009|newspaper=Yorkshire Evening Post|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171009143648/http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-wildcats-winterstein-poised-for-crusaders-1-2232538|archive-date=9 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/5090961/Squeaky-clean-Winterstein-eager-to-make-mark-for-Wakefield.html |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London |title='Squeaky clean' Winterstein eager to make mark for Wakefield |first=Michael |last=Fisher |date=2009-04-01 |access-date=2010-05-02}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/warriors-and-wolves-silent-over-future-of-gleeson-1659800.html |work=The Independent |location=London |title=Warriors and Wolves silent over future of Gleeson |first=Dave |last=Hadfield |date=2009-04-02 |access-date=2010-05-02}}</ref>

Winterstein later signed a two-year deal at Super League newcomers [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]].

===Bradford Bulls===
In October 2013, Winterstein signed a one-year deal with the [[Bradford Bulls]] while he was playing in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] with Samoa.<ref>{{cite web |date=29 October 2013 |title=Frank Winterstein: Bradford Bulls sign Samoa's ex-Widnes forward |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24706938 |publisher=BBC |access-date=9 February 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Wilson |first=Jack |date=29 October 2013 |title=Bradford snap up Samoan forward Frank Winterstein |url=https://www.dailystar.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/347892/Bradford-snap-up-Samoan-forward-Frank-Winterstein |work=Daily Star|location=United Kingdom|access-date=9 February 2018}}</ref> He was released by mutual consent in January 2014, without having appeared for the club.<ref>{{cite web |date=6 January 2014 |title=Frank Winterstein released by Bradford Bulls |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/25620468 |publisher=BBC |access-date=9 February 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=6 January 2014 |title=Bradford Bulls release Winterstein |url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/bradford-bulls-release-winterstein |publisher=SBS |access-date=9 February 2018}}</ref>

===Rugby union===
In March 2016, Winterstein joined the [[Australia national rugby sevens team|Australian rugby sevens squad]].<ref>{{cite web |date=18 March 2016 |title=Winterstein joins Australian Sevens program |url=https://www.rugby.com.au/news/2016/03/21/winterstein-joins-australian-sevens-program |publisher=rugby.com.au |access-date=9 February 2018}}</ref> He appeared at the [[2016 Hong Kong Sevens]], [[2016 Singapore Sevens]] and [[2016 London Sevens]].<ref name=itsrugby />

===Manly Warringah Sea Eagles===
Winterstein returned to rugby league in August 2016, signing with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] effective immediately until the end of 2017.<ref name="auto">{{cite web |date=20 August 2016 |title=Manly sign Frank Winterstein |url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2016/08/20/manly_sign_frank_win.html |publisher=seaeagles.com.au |access-date=8 October 2017}}</ref> In July 2017, Winterstein extended his contract with the Sea Eagles until the end of 2019.<ref>{{cite web |date=11 July 2017 |title=Winterstein signs new Manly NRL deal |url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/winterstein-signs-new-manly-nrl-deal |publisher=SBS |access-date=9 February 2018}}</ref>

===Penrith Panthers===
At the start of the [[2019 NRL season]], Winterstein was released by Manly-Warringah and he signed a contract to join Penrith.  Winterstein made his debut for Penrith in round two against Newcastle, scoring a try in a 16–14 victory at the [[Newcastle International Sports Centre]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/03/11/frank-winterstein-set-for-late-penrith-switch/|title=Winterstein set for Penrith switch after Manly release|website=NRL}}</ref>

On 16 September 2019, it was revealed that Winterstein was one of ten players who were to be released by the Penrith club at the end of the [[2019 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2019/09/16/2019-merv-cartwright-medal-tribute-to-departing-players/|title=Tribute to Departing Players|website=Penrith Panthers}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
At the end of the 2019 season, the 32-year-old was not offered another Australian contract. He then signed a two-year deal with the French rugby league team [[Toulouse Olympique]] for the European 2020/21 seasons.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Anti-vax WAG Taylor Winterstein claims there is a 'strong core' of anti-vaxxing families in the NRL|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/entertainment/sydney-confidential/antivax-wag-taylor-winterstein-claims-healthcare-workers-are-lying-and-injecting-bleach-is-akin-to-vaccination/news-story/ca741b778cc966ae09698e5ac8f9f69b|last=Coates|first=Sally|date=2020-05-06|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|url-access=subscription|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20200506124502/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/entertainment/sydney-confidential/antivax-wag-taylor-winterstein-claims-healthcare-workers-are-lying-and-injecting-bleach-is-akin-to-vaccination/news-story/ca741b778cc966ae09698e5ac8f9f69b|archive-date=6 May 2020|access-date=6 May 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/frank-winterstein-joins-toulouse/|title=Frank Winterstein joins Toulouse|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=2019-10-24|work=Love Rugby League|location=UK|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191223051501/https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/frank-winterstein-joins-toulouse/|archive-date=2019-12-23}}</ref>

On 22 Jan 2021, it was reported that he had left the club by mutual consent<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 January 2021|access-date=23 January 2021|title=Frank Winterstein leaves Toulouse|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/frank-winterstein-leaves-toulouse/}}</ref>

===Representative career===
Winterstein has represented [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] internationally, first playing in the [[Federation Shield]] in 2005.<ref name="England 38-14 Samoa">{{cite news |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/6103128.stm |title=England 38-14 Samoa |publisher=BBC |date=2006-11-05 |access-date=2008-07-30}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Winterstein is the cousin of [[North Queensland Cowboys]] winger [[Antonio Winterstein]].<ref name="auto"/> He is married to social media influencer [[Taylor Winterstein]].<ref name="miracle">{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/lifestyle/health/nrl-star-frank-wintersteins-wife-taylor-selling-miracle-powder-she-claims-cures-illness-for-150-a-pop/news-story/54db7fbb34cefcb5b4dcd5e31a0bba7e|title=Doctor lashes anti-vaxxer's 'miracle' cure|last=Molloy|first=Shannon|date=2019-04-19|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|language=en|url-status=live|archive-url=https://archive.today/20191202215830/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/lifestyle/health/nrl-star-frank-wintersteins-wife-taylor-selling-miracle-powder-she-claims-cures-illness-for-150-a-pop/news-story/54db7fbb34cefcb5b4dcd5e31a0bba7e|archive-date=2 December 2019}}</ref>
